title: Chemical splash eye-exposure first response
summary: Move away from the source, begin immediate gentle rinsing with clean water when safe, remove contact lenses if they come out easily, and call emergency or poison guidance.
content: Move away from the source, begin immediate gentle rinsing with clean water when safe, remove contact lenses if they come out easily, and call emergency or poison guidance. Bring the product label or a photo without delaying rinsing or exposing another person. Do not neutralize the chemical with another chemical or rub the eye.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Chemical splash eye-exposure first response”.

short_term: For Chemical splash eye-exposure first response, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Chemical splash eye-exposure first response, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Chemical splash eye-exposure first response, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
